title: OpenCut: a fast-growing open-source video editor with an agent-ready roadmap
friendlyTitle: OpenCut: a free video editor that plans to work with AI agents
summary: OpenCut is an open-source video editor for web, desktop, and mobile. Its active rewrite plans a Rust core, plugin-first architecture, headless batch rendering, scripting, and an MCP server for AI agents; the project has roughly 65,800 GitHub stars.
friendlySummary: OpenCut is a free, open-source video editor for the web, desktop, and mobile. Its next version plans features for scripts, batch video jobs, and AI agents.
whyItMatters: Creators and developers can follow or contribute to a local-first editing stack with automation in mind. The current classic version is the one the maintainers recommend for production use while the rewrite is in progress.
friendlyWhyItMatters: It is worth watching if you want an open video editor that may automate repeated editing jobs. Use the classic version today if you need a stable tool.
